data.content:
Now I have the full picture. Here are all the **legacy patterns** found:

| # | Legacy Pattern | Modernization |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="...">` | → `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">` |
| 2 | Explicit `Microsoft.Common.props` import | Implicit in SDK |
| 3 | Explicit `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` import | Implicit in SDK |
| 4 | `TargetFrameworkVersion` v4.7.2 | → `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>` |
| 5 | `ProjectGuid` | Not needed |
| 6 | `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects` | SDK defaults |
| 7 | Debug/Release `PropertyGroup` blocks | SDK provides sensible defaults |
| 8 | Explicit `<Reference>` for System assemblies | Implicit with SDK |
| 9 | Explicit `<Compile Include="...">` items | SDK uses file globbing |
| 10 | `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Replaced by project-level properties |

Let me apply the modernization now.

arguments.old_str:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" Condition="Exists('$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props')" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <Configuration Condition=" '$(Configuration)' == '' ">Debug</Configuration>
    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>
    <ProjectGuid>{12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012}</ProjectGuid>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <FileAlignment>512</FileAlignment>
    <AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>
    <Deterministic>true</Deterministic>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Debug|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
    <DebugType>full</DebugType>
    <Optimize>false</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Debug\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>DEBUG;TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Release|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugType>pdbonly</DebugType>
    <Optimize>true</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Release\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="System" />
    <Reference Include="System.Core" />
    <Reference Include="System.Data" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>
arguments.new_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a complete picture. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and modernize the project.

Legacy patterns found:
1. **Old-style `<Project>` declaration** with `ToolsVersion` and XML namespace — SDK-style uses `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">`
2. **Explicit `Microsoft.Common.props` import** — handled automatically by SDK
3. **Explicit `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` import** — handled automatically by SDK
4. **Configuration/Platform property groups** with Debug/Release defaults — SDK provides sensible defaults
5. **`ProjectGuid`** — not needed in SDK-style


6. **`FileAlignment`**, **`AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`**, and **`Deterministic`** — all defaults in modern SDK
7. **Debug/Release PropertyGroups** — SDK already provides reasonable defaults for output paths, debug types, and constants
8. **Explicit System assembly references** — implicit when targeting .NET Framework via SDK
9. **Manual `<Compile>` item declarations** — SDK uses globbing patterns automatically
10. **`Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs`** — auto-generated by SDK-style projects

Now I'm converting the legacy project to SDK-style. The `TargetFrameworkVersion` of v4.7.2 becomes `net472` in the modern format, and I can move assembly metadata directly into the project properties instead of maintaining a separate AssemblyInfo file. I'm building the modernized project file with the SDK structure...

Finishing the project file...

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" Condition="Exists('$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props')" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <Configuration Condition=" '$(Configuration)' == '' ">Debug</Configuration>
    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>
    <ProjectGuid>{12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012}</ProjectGuid>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <FileAlignment>512</FileAlignment>
    <AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>
    <Deterministic>true</Deterministic>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Debug|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
    <DebugType>full</DebugType>
    <Optimize>false</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Debug\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>DEBUG;TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Release|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugType>pdbonly</DebugType>
    <Optimize>true</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Release\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="System" />
    <Reference Include="System.Core" />
    <Reference Include="System.Data" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>

<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

data.content:
Done. The project went from **47 lines → 11 lines**. Here's a summary of what changed:

**Removed (handled by SDK defaults):**
- XML declaration, `ToolsVersion`, `xmlns` namespace
- `Microsoft.Common.props` / `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` imports
- `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`
- Debug/Release configuration blocks (OutputPath, DebugType, DefineConstants, etc.)
- Explicit `<Reference>` items for System assemblies
- Explicit `<Compile Include>` items (SDK globs `**/*.cs` automatically)
- `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` — metadata moved to `<PropertyGroup>` properties

**Retained (meaningful project-specific values):**
- `TargetFramework` (net472), `Description`, `Company`, `Copyright`, `Version`
- `OutputType` and `RootNamespace`/`AssemblyName` omitted since `Library` and `LegacyApp` are the SDK defaults for this project name.
